# Scalars

| Name | Detail Count | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Boolean](boolean-scalar.md) | 0 | The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`. |
| [DateTime](date_time-scalar.md) | 0 | Represents a date and time in ISO 8601 format. Accepts shortcuts like `2021` to represent midnight Fri Jan 01 2021. Also accepts ISO 8601 durations strings which are added to the current date to create the represented date (e.g '-P2W1D' represents the date that was two weeks and 1 day ago)  |
| [DateTimeOrDuration](date_time_or_duration-scalar.md) | 0 | Represents a date and time in ISO 8601 format. Accepts shortcuts like `2021` to represent midnight Fri Jan 01 2021. Also accepts ISO 8601 durations strings which are added to the current date to create the represented date (e.g '-P2W1D' represents the date that was two weeks and 1 day ago)  |
| [Duration](duration-scalar.md) | 0 | Represents a duration in ISO 8601 format. Accepts ISO 8601 duration strings or integers in milliseconds. |
| [Float](float-scalar.md) | 0 | The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point). |
| [ID](id-scalar.md) | 0 | The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer (such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID. |
| [Int](int-scalar.md) | 0 | The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1. |
| [IssueAssignedToYouNotificationType](issue_assigned_to_you_notification_type-scalar.md) | 0 | An issue assignment notification type. |
| [IssueCommentMentionNotificationType](issue_comment_mention_notification_type-scalar.md) | 0 | An issue comment mention notification type. |
| [IssueCommentReactionNotificationType](issue_comment_reaction_notification_type-scalar.md) | 0 | An issue comment reaction notification type. |
| [IssueEmojiReactionNotificationType](issue_emoji_reaction_notification_type-scalar.md) | 0 | An issue emoji reaction notification type. |
| [IssueMentionNotificationType](issue_mention_notification_type-scalar.md) | 0 | An issue mention notification type. |
| [IssueNewCommentNotificationType](issue_new_comment_notification_type-scalar.md) | 0 | An issue new comment notification type. |
| [IssueStatusChangedNotificationType](issue_status_changed_notification_type-scalar.md) | 0 | An issue status changed notification type. |
| [IssueUnassignedFromYouNotificationType](issue_unassigned_from_you_notification_type-scalar.md) | 0 | An issue unassignment notification type. |
| [JSON](json-scalar.md) | 0 | The `JSON` scalar type represents arbitrary values as *stringified* JSON |
| [JSONObject](json_object-scalar.md) | 0 | The `JSONObject` scalar type represents arbitrary values as *embedded* JSON |
| [String](string-scalar.md) | 0 | The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text. |
| [TimelessDate](timeless_date-scalar.md) | 0 | Represents a date in ISO 8601 format. Accepts shortcuts like `2021` to represent midnight Fri Jan 01 2021. Also accepts ISO 8601 durations strings which are added to the current date to create the represented date (e.g '-P2W1D' represents the date that was two weeks and 1 day ago)  |
| [TimelessDateOrDuration](timeless_date_or_duration-scalar.md) | 0 | Represents a date in ISO 8601 format or a duration. Accepts shortcuts like `2021` to represent midnight Fri Jan 01 2021. Also accepts ISO 8601 durations strings (e.g '-P2W1D'), which are not converted to dates. |
| [UUID](uuid-scalar.md) | 0 | A universally unique identifier as specified by RFC 4122. |
